label: AWS security credentials
name: awsApi
description: Your AWS security credentials. When unspecified, credentials will be sourced from the runtime environment according to the default AWS SDK behavior.

inputs.label: AWS Access Key
inputs.name: awsKey
inputs.description: The access key for your AWS account.

inputs.label: AWS Secret Access Key
inputs.name: awsSecret
inputs.description: The secret key for your AWS account.

inputs.label: AWS Session Key
inputs.name: awsSession
inputs.description: The session key for your AWS account. This is only needed when you are using temporary credentials.

inputs.label: Azure Subscription Key
inputs.name: azureSubscriptionKey
inputs.description: Your Azure Cognitive Services subscription key

inputs.label: Service Region
inputs.name: serviceRegion
inputs.description: The Azure service region (e.g., "westus", "eastus")

inputs.label: API Version
inputs.name: apiVersion
inputs.description: The API version to use (e.g., "2024-05-15-preview")

name: azureFoundryApi

inputs.label: Azure Foundry API Key
inputs.name: azureFoundryApiKey
inputs.description: Your Azure AI Foundry API key

inputs.label: Azure Foundry Endpoint
inputs.name: azureFoundryEndpoint
inputs.description: Your Azure AI Foundry endpoint URL

name: azureOpenAIApi
description: Refer to official guide of how to use Azure OpenAI service

inputs.label: Azure OpenAI Api Key
inputs.name: azureOpenAIApiKey
inputs.description: Refer to official guide on how to create API key on Azure OpenAI

inputs.label: Azure OpenAI Api Version
inputs.name: azureOpenAIApiVersion
inputs.description: Description of Supported API Versions. Please refer examples

inputs.label: Google Application Credential File Path
inputs.name: googleApplicationCredentialFilePath
inputs.description: Path to your google application credential json file. You can also use the credential JSON object (either one)

inputs.label: Google Credential JSON Object
inputs.name: googleApplicationCredential
inputs.description: JSON object of your google application credential. You can also use the file path (either one)

inputs.label: Project ID
inputs.name: projectID
inputs.description: Project ID of GCP. If not provided, it will be read from the credential file

label: Microsoft Outlook OAuth2
name: microsoftOutlookOAuth2
description: You can find the setup instructions here

inputs.label: Scope
inputs.name: scope
inputs.default: openid offline_access Contacts.Read Contacts.ReadWrite Calendars.Read Calendars.Read.Shared Calendars.ReadWrite Mail.Read Mail.ReadWrite Mail.ReadWrite.Shared Mail.Send Mail.Send.Shared MailboxSettings.Read

label: Microsoft Teams OAuth2
name: microsoftTeamsOAuth2
description: You can find the setup instructions here

inputs.label: Scope
inputs.name: scope
inputs.default: openid offline_access User.Read User.ReadWrite.All Group.ReadWrite.All Team.ReadBasic.All Team.Create TeamMember.ReadWrite.All Channel.ReadBasic.All Channel.Create Channel.Delete.All ChannelMember.ReadWrite.All Chat.ReadWrite Chat.Create ChatMember.ReadWrite ChatMessage.Send ChatMessage.Read ChannelMessage.Send ChannelMessage.Read.All TeamsActivity.Send
